findings.title: CSS transform order is wrong: scale then translate produces incorrect pan
findings.description: buildTransformStyle returns `scale(...) translate(...)`. CSS transforms are applied right-to-left (innermost first), so this translates in the original coordinate space, then scales — meaning the translation distances are NOT scaled. The intended behaviour is almost certainly to scale around a focal point, which requires the opposite order: `translate(...) scale(...)`, so the translation happens in the already-scaled space. With the current order, zooming into a station will pan by the raw translateX/Y values without accounting for scale, putting the viewport in the wrong place at every non-overview zoom level.
findings.fix:
Swap the order: `return \`translate(${zoom.translateX}px, ${zoom.translateY}px) scale(${zoom.scale})\`;`

Alternatively, if the geometry pipeline pre-compensates for this, document it explicitly with a comment — but the conventional approach for zoom-to-point is translate-then-scale.

findings.title: lineToGroup does not cover sauces_condiments or herbs_spices — falls through to "seasoning" silently
findings.description: The function uses a chain of if/if/if with a bare `return "seasoning"` fallback. This works today because the only remaining FoodLine values are sauces_condiments and herbs_spices, both of which belong to the seasoning group. However the function is not exhaustive — any future addition of a FoodLine that is NOT seasoning will silently return "seasoning" instead of causing a compile-time error. TypeScript cannot catch this because the return type is just `FoodGroup`, not a discriminated union that forces exhaustion. The risk is a silent mis-grouping that corrupts zoom breadcrumb context.

findings.title: zoomOut closure captures stale context via dependency array
findings.description: The `zoomOut` callback declares `[currentZoom.level, context.line, context.corridor, geometry]` as its dependencies. This reads the primitive values at the time `useCallback` runs, so the closure should be fresh — this is not a classic stale-closure bug. However, the pattern of listing destructured primitives from state objects (rather than the whole state objects) is fragile: if a future maintainer adds `context.station` to the switch without updating the dep array, the closure will silently read stale station data. The dependency list is also slightly misaligned — `context.line` and `context.corridor` are already covered by listing `context` directly, and listing the whole `context` object would be safer and no more expensive.

findings.title: Initial zoom state is not reset when geometry prop changes identity
findings.description: The hook initialises `currentZoom` from `geometry.zoomLevels.overview` via `useState`. If the parent component ever passes a new `geometry` object (e.g. after a data reload or React StrictMode double-render in development), the state is not re-synchronised — the hook will continue using the stale initial zoom while calling `geometry.zoomLevels.corridor(...)` on the new geometry object. This is a latent inconsistency. In practice `geometry` is auto-generated static data that never changes, but the hook provides no safety net.

findings.title: transformStyle and target are recomputed every render, unstable references returned
findings.description: `target` and `transformStyle` are plain string derivations computed inline on every render. This is not a bug, but both values are returned in the hook's return object, which is itself a new object reference every render. Any consumer that spreads or destructures the return value and passes sub-values to memoised children will not be protected from re-renders triggered by unrelated state. The `transformStyle` string is the value most likely to be passed directly to a style prop on an SVG element, where referential stability matters for paint-optimisation.

findings.title: zoomToStation silently accepts unknown canonicals without error feedback
findings.description: If `findStationLine` returns `undefined` (canonical not found in geometry), `zoomToStation` still calls `geometry.zoomLevels.station(canonical)` with an unknown key. Whether this throws, returns a default, or silently renders an incorrect transform depends entirely on the geometry implementation. The hook itself gives no feedback to the caller. For a debug/development scenario this is silent misbehaviour.
